opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I'm in absolute agony — the pain in my side is worse than anything I've ever felt and I can't stop throwing up. It woke me out of my sleep a couple of hours ago and I can't sit still, I keep pacing and writhing trying to find any position that helps and nothing does.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain came on suddenly out of nowhere and it's unbearable, I've vomited up everything including the paracetamol I tried, and I can't keep any water down.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden, about two hours ago — it woke me from sleep
symptom_narrative.character: excruciating, gripping pain in one side of my back and flank that shoots round to the front towards my groin and testicle
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst pain of my life — I cannot keep still, I'm pacing and rolling around
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: one side, in the back/flank, radiating round to the groin
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant with waves that get even worse
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — I can't find any position that eases it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps coming in waves on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: severe nausea and repeated vomiting; I can't keep water or tablets down at all
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely unable to function — I can only pace and writhe

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: at home alone when the pain woke him; well until this episode

objective_data_offered.value: constantly pacing and writhing on the video, unable to sit or stay still for more than a moment, looks pale and is drenched in sweat, visibly in severe distress
I saw blood in my urine once, just before the pain got to its worst
I've thrown up several times and there are emesis basins next to me; I can't keep anything down
